seoTitle: Send PayPal Money Without a Linked Bank Account (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to fund and send PayPal payments without a bank account using prepaid cards, retail cash-in, or your existing balance.
intro: You don't need to attach a traditional bank account to send money through PayPal. This guide covers alternative ways to fund your wallet and complete a peer-to-peer transfer using just your balance or a prepaid card. It's designed for anyone who prefers to keep banking details off the platform entirely.

steps.heading: Fund your wallet an alternate way
steps.body: Load your balance using a linked prepaid gift card, retail cash-in at a local store, or an incoming transfer from someone else.

steps.heading: Open Send & Request
steps.body: Click the Send & Request icon in the main menu to start a new transfer.

steps.heading: Enter the recipient's details
steps.body: Type your recipient's verified PayPal email address or mobile number, then input the amount and currency.

steps.heading: Choose the right transfer type
steps.body: Select Friends and Family for a personal transfer or Goods and Services if you need buyer protection.

steps.heading: Select your funding source and send
steps.body: Toggle your funding source to your PayPal Balance or linked prepaid card, then confirm to send the payment.

faq.question: Can I really send money with zero bank linkage?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as your balance is funded through cash-in, a prepaid card, or an incoming transfer, no bank account is required.

faq.question: Which transfer type should I choose for a friend?
faq.answer: Friends and Family is meant for personal transfers and generally has no fee when funded by balance or bank.

faq.question: Are there fees using a prepaid card as the funding source?
faq.answer: Card-funded transfers can carry a small fee, so check the fee breakdown shown before confirming.

faq.question: What if the recipient's email is unverified?
faq.answer: The payment may show as pending until the recipient verifies their account, so double-check the email before sending.
